   482	/** Handle memory_search tool — performs hybrid vector/BM25 search with intent-aware ranking.
   483	 * @param args - Search arguments (query, concepts, mode, specFolder, etc.)
   484	 * @returns MCP response with ranked search results
   485	 */
   486	async function handleMemorySearch(args: SearchArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   487	  const _searchStartTime = Date.now();
   488	  resetLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot();
   489	  // BUG-001: Check for external database updates before processing
   490	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   491	
   492	  const {
   493	    cursor,
   494	    query,
   495	    concepts,
   496	    specFolder,
   497	    folderBoost,
   498	    tenantId,
   499	    userId,
   500	    agentId,
   501	    sharedSpaceId,
   502	    limit: rawLimit = 10,
   503	    tier,
   504	    contextType,
   505	    useDecay: useDecay = true,
   506	    includeContiguity: includeContiguity = false,
   507	    includeConstitutional: includeConstitutional = true,
   508	    includeContent: includeContent = false,
   509	    anchors,
   510	    bypassCache: bypassCache = false,
   511	    sessionId,
   512	    enableDedup: enableDedup = true,
   513	    intent: explicitIntent,
   514	    autoDetectIntent: autoDetectIntent = true,
   515	    minState,  // No default — memoryState column not yet in schema; defaulting to 'WARM' filters all rows
   516	    applyStateLimits: applyStateLimits = false,
   517	    rerank = true, // Enable reranking by default for better result quality
   518	    applyLengthPenalty: applyLengthPenalty = true,
   519	    trackAccess: trackAccess = false, // opt-in, off by default
   520	    includeArchived: includeArchived = false, // REQ-206: exclude archived by default
   521	    enableSessionBoost: enableSessionBoost = isSessionBoostEnabled(),
   522	    enableCausalBoost: enableCausalBoost = isCausalBoostEnabled(),
   523	    minQualityScore,
   524	    min_quality_score,
   525	    mode,
   526	    includeTrace: includeTraceArg = false,
   527	    sessionTransition,
   528	    profile,
   529	    retrievalLevel: retrievalLevel = 'auto',
   530	  } = args;
   531	  const includeTraceByFlag = process.env.SPECKIT_RESPONSE_TRACE === 'true';
   532	  const includeTrace = includeTraceByFlag || includeTraceArg === true;
   533	  const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext({ tenantId, userId, agentId, sessionId, sharedSpaceId });
   534	  const progressiveScopeKey = JSON.stringify({
   535	    tenantId: normalizedScope.tenantId ?? null,
   536	    userId: normalizedScope.userId ?? null,
   537	    agentId: normalizedScope.agentId ?? null,
   538	    sessionId: normalizedScope.sessionId ?? null,
   539	    sharedSpaceId: normalizedScope.sharedSpaceId ?? null,
   540	  });
   541	
   542	  // Validate at least one search input is provided (moved from schema superRefine for GPT compatibility)
   543	  const hasCursor = typeof cursor === 'string' && cursor.trim().length > 0;
   544	  const hasQuery = typeof query === 'string' && query.trim().length > 0;
   545	  const hasConcepts = Array.isArray(concepts) && concepts.length >= 2;
   546	  if (!hasCursor && !hasQuery && !hasConcepts) {
   547	    return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Either "query" (string), "concepts" (array with 2-5 items), or "cursor" (string) is required.' }) }] };
   548	  }
   549	
   550	  if (hasCursor) {
   551	    const resolved = resolveCursor(cursor.trim(), undefined, { scopeKey: progressiveScopeKey });
   552	    if (!resolved) {
   553	      return createMCPErrorResponse({
   554	        tool: 'memory_search',
   555	        error: 'Cursor is invalid, expired, or out of scope',
   556	        code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   557	        details: { parameter: 'cursor' },
   558	        recovery: {
   559	          hint: 'Retry the original search to generate a fresh continuation cursor',
   560	        },
   561	      });
   562	    }
   563	
   564	    const snippetResults = extractSnippets(resolved.results);
   565	    return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   566	      tool: 'memory_search',
   567	      summary: `Returned ${snippetResults.length} continuation results`,
   568	      data: {
   569	        count: snippetResults.length,
   570	        results: snippetResults,
   571	        continuation: resolved.continuation,
   572	      },
   573	      startTime: _searchStartTime,
   574	      cacheHit: false,
   575	    });
   576	  }
   577	
   578	  const qualityThreshold = resolveQualityThreshold(minQualityScore, min_quality_score);
   579	
   580	  // Validate numeric limit parameter
   581	  const limit = (typeof rawLimit === 'number' && Number.isFinite(rawLimit) && rawLimit > 0)
   582	    ? Math.min(Math.floor(rawLimit), 100)
   583	    : 10;
   584	
   585	  // BUG-007: Validate query first with proper error handling
   586	  let normalizedQuery: string | null = null;
   587	  if (query !== undefined) {
   588	    try {
   589	      normalizedQuery = validateQuery(query);
   590	    } catch (validationError: unknown) {
   591	      if (!concepts || !Array.isArray(concepts) || concepts.length < 2) {
   592	        const message = toErrorMessage(validationError);
   593	        return createMCPErrorResponse({
   594	          tool: 'memory_search',
   595	          error: message,
   596	          code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   597	          details: { parameter: 'query' },
   598	          recovery: {
   599	            hint: 'Provide a valid query string or use concepts array instead'
   600	          }
   601	        });
   602	      }
   603	      normalizedQuery = null;
   604	    }
   605	  }
   606	
   607	  const hasValidQuery = normalizedQuery !== null;
   608	  const hasValidConcepts = Array.isArray(concepts) && concepts.length >= 2;
   609	  const effectiveQuery = normalizedQuery ?? (hasValidConcepts ? concepts.join(', ') : '');
   610	
   611	  if (!hasValidQuery && !hasValidConcepts) {
   612	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   613	      tool: 'memory_search',
   614	      error: 'Either query (string), concepts (array of 2-5 strings), or cursor (string) is required',
   615	      code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   616	      details: { parameter: 'query' },
   617	      recovery: {
   618	        hint: 'Provide a query string, concepts array with 2-5 entries, or a continuation cursor'
   619	      }
   620	    });
   621	  }
   622	
   623	  if (specFolder !== undefined && typeof specFolder !== 'string') {
   624	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   625	      tool: 'memory_search',
   626	      error: 'specFolder must be a string',
   627	      code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   628	      details: { parameter: 'specFolder' },
   629	      recovery: {
   630	        hint: 'Provide specFolder as a string path'
   631	      }
   632	    });
   633	  }
   634	
   635	  // Eval logger — capture query at pipeline entry (fail-safe)
   636	  let _evalQueryId = 0;
   637	  let _evalRunId = 0;
   638	  try {
   639	    const evalEntry = logSearchQuery({
   640	      query: effectiveQuery,
   641	      intent: explicitIntent ?? null,
   642	      specFolder: specFolder ?? null,
   643	    });
   644	    _evalQueryId = evalEntry.queryId;
   645	    _evalRunId = evalEntry.evalRunId;
   646	  } catch (_error: unknown) { /* eval logging must never break search */ }
   647	
   648	  const artifactRoutingQuery = resolveArtifactRoutingQuery(
   649	    normalizedQuery,
   650	    hasValidConcepts ? concepts : undefined
   651	  );
   652	  let artifactRouting = getStrategyForQuery(artifactRoutingQuery, specFolder);
   653	
   654	  // Intent-aware retrieval
   655	  let detectedIntent: string | null = null;
   656	  let intentConfidence = 0;
   657	  let intentWeights: IntentWeights | null = null;
   658	
   659	  if (explicitIntent) {
   660	    if (intentClassifier.isValidIntent(explicitIntent)) {
   661	      detectedIntent = explicitIntent;
   662	      intentConfidence = 1.0;
   663	      intentWeights = intentClassifier.getIntentWeights(explicitIntent);
   664	    } else {
   665	      console.warn(`[memory-search] Invalid intent '${explicitIntent}', using auto-detection`);
   666	    }
   667	  }
   668	
   669	  if (!detectedIntent && autoDetectIntent && hasValidQuery) {
   670	    const classification: IntentClassification = intentClassifier.classifyIntent(normalizedQuery!);
   671	    detectedIntent = classification.intent;
   672	    intentConfidence = classification.confidence;
   673	    intentWeights = intentClassifier.getIntentWeights(classification.intent as IntentType);
   674	
   675	    if (classification.fallback) {
   676	      console.error(`[memory-search] Intent auto-detected as '${detectedIntent}' (fallback: ${classification.reason})`);
   677	    } else {
   678	      console.error(`[memory-search] Intent auto-detected as '${detectedIntent}' (confidence: ${intentConfidence.toFixed(2)})`);
   679	    }
   680	  }
   681	
   682	  // FIX RC3-B: Intent confidence floor — override low-confidence auto-detections to "understand"
   683	  const INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = parseFloat(process.env.SPECKIT_INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR || '0.25');
   684	  if (detectedIntent && intentConfidence < INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR && !explicitIntent) {
   685	    console.error(`[memory-search] Intent confidence ${intentConfidence.toFixed(3)} below floor ${INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR}, overriding '${detectedIntent}' → 'understand'`);
   686	    detectedIntent = 'understand';
   687	    intentConfidence = 1.0;
   688	    intentWeights = intentClassifier.getIntentWeights('understand' as IntentType);
   689	  }
   690	
   691	  // Phase C: Intent-to-profile auto-routing.
   692	  // Explicit caller `profile` always takes precedence; auto-detect fills in when absent.
   693	  let effectiveProfile: string | undefined = profile;
   694	  if (!effectiveProfile && detectedIntent && isIntentAutoProfileEnabled()) {
   695	    try {
   696	      const autoProfile = intentClassifier.getProfileForIntent(detectedIntent as IntentType);
   697	      if (autoProfile) {
   698	        effectiveProfile = autoProfile;
   699	        console.error(`[memory-search] Intent-to-profile auto-routing: '${detectedIntent}' → profile '${autoProfile}'`);
   700	      }
   701	    } catch (_autoProfileErr: unknown) {
   702	      // Auto-profile is best-effort — never breaks search
   703	    }
   704	  }
   705	
   706	  // Re-run artifact routing with detected intent for fallback coverage
   707	  if (detectedIntent && artifactRouting?.detectedClass === 'unknown' && artifactRouting?.confidence === 0) {
   708	    artifactRouting = getStrategyForQuery(artifactRoutingQuery, specFolder, detectedIntent);
   709	  }
   710	
   711	  // Create retrieval trace at pipeline entry
   712	  const trace = createTrace(
   713	    effectiveQuery,
   714	    sessionId,
   715	    detectedIntent || undefined
   716	  );
   717	
   718	  // Build cache key args
   719	  const cacheArgs = buildCacheArgs({
   720	    normalizedQuery,
   721	    hasValidConcepts,
   722	    concepts,
   723	    specFolder,
   724	    tenantId: normalizedScope.tenantId,
   725	    userId: normalizedScope.userId,
   726	    agentId: normalizedScope.agentId,
   727	    sharedSpaceId: normalizedScope.sharedSpaceId,
   728	    limit,
   729	    mode,
   730	    tier,
   731	    contextType,
   732	    useDecay,
   733	    includeArchived,
   734	    qualityThreshold,
   735	    applyStateLimits,
   736	    includeContiguity,
   737	    includeConstitutional,
   738	    includeContent,
   739	    anchors,
   740	    detectedIntent,
   741	    minState: minState ?? '',
   742	    rerank,
   743	    applyLengthPenalty,
   744	    sessionId,
   745	    enableSessionBoost,
   746	    enableCausalBoost,
   747	    includeTrace,
   748	  });
   749	
   750	  let _evalChannelPayloads: EvalChannelPayload[] = [];
   751	  const cacheKey = toolCache.generateCacheKey('memory_search', cacheArgs);
   752	  const cacheEnabled = toolCache.isEnabled() && !bypassCache;
   753	  const cachedPayload = cacheEnabled
   754	    ? toolCache.get<SearchCachePayload>(cacheKey)
   755	    : null;
   756	
   757	  let responseToReturn: MCPResponse;
   758	  let goalRefinementPayload: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
   759	
   760	  if (cachedPayload) {
   761	    responseToReturn = buildSearchResponseFromPayload(cachedPayload, _searchStartTime, true);
   762	  } else {
   763	    // Wait for embedding model only on cache miss
   764	    if (!isEmbeddingModelReady()) {
   765	      const modelReady = await waitForEmbeddingModel(30000);
   766	      if (!modelReady) {
   767	        throw new Error('Embedding model not ready after 30s timeout. Try again later.');
   768	      }
   769	    }
   770	
   771	    // V2 pipeline is the only path (legacy V1 removed from the runtime pipeline)
   772	    const pipelineConfig: PipelineConfig = {
   773	      query: effectiveQuery,
   774	      concepts: hasValidConcepts ? concepts : undefined,
   775	      searchType: (hasValidConcepts && concepts!.length >= 2)
   776	        ? 'multi-concept'
   777	        : 'hybrid',
   778	      mode,
   779	      limit,
   780	      specFolder,
   781	      tenantId: normalizedScope.tenantId,
   782	      userId: normalizedScope.userId,

   785	      tier,

   797	      sessionId,

    17	### Finding 4: Public’s private vs shared scoping is first-class and enforced at both save-time and retrieval-time
    18	- **Source**: [scope-governance.ts:456](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L456), [scope-governance.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L482), [memory-save.ts:1164](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1164), [shared-spaces.ts:555](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts#L555), [create-record.ts:108](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L108)
    19	- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed
    20	- **What it does**: saves and reads carry `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`; shared rows use membership as the visibility boundary; shared saves require editor access; same-path dedup and existing-row lookup are scope-aware instead of repo-wide.
    21	- **Why it matters**: this is the right answer to “shared vs private” memory. Public does not just have a global bucket; it has explicit private scope, collaborator scope, and scoped dedup so one actor’s memory does not accidentally collapse another actor’s record.
    22	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
    23	- **Affected subsystem**: governed retrieval, shared-memory writes, duplicate suppression
    24	- **Impact**: high

    26	### Finding 5: Public has explicit DB-level concurrency controls, but shared-memory admin auth still trusts transport-supplied identity
    27	- **Source**: [vector-index-store.ts:802](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L802), [vector-index-store.ts:803](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L803), [context-server.ts:1632](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1632), [session-manager.ts:603](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/session-manager.ts#L603), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:237](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L237), [shared-memory.ts:232](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/shared-memory.ts#L232), [shared-memory.ts:245](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/shared-memory.ts#L245)

    29	- **What it does**: Public forces WAL, sets `busy_timeout`, uses `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for session dedup races, and wraps reconsolidation/save paths in transactions. But shared-memory admin mutations still warn that actor IDs are caller-supplied and not yet cryptographically bound to an authenticated transport principal.
    30	- **Why it matters**: concurrency at the SQLite layer is meaningfully stronger in Public than anything the Mnemosyne plugin proves. The remaining gap is not write atomicity; it is identity trust for collaborative admin operations.

    33	- **Impact**: high

    36	- New information ratio: 0.44
    37	- The external repo does not prove multi-agent safety beyond CLI invocation hygiene and simple project/global scoping. The stronger answer is that Spec Kit Memory already contains most of the needed safety model: explicit scope metadata, membership-gated shared spaces, append-version conflict recording, manual-merge escalation, lineage-preserving writes, and transactional protections.

    42	--- Iteration 26 ---
    43	## Findings
    44	- **Why it matters**: This is the right architecture for long-lived memory. Permanence, recall probability, and current-session salience should stay independent so we do not accidentally make durable rules feel “old” or recent noise feel “important.”

    48	### Finding 3: Reversible forgetting is stronger than hard deletion for durable memory
    49	- **Source**: [archival-manager.ts:358](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L358), [archival-manager.ts:388](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L388), [archival-manager.ts:403](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L403), [archival-manager.ts:558](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L558), [archival-manager.ts:638](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L638), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L396); evidence type: wrapper-confirmed
    50	- **What it does**: Public archives low-value memories instead of immediately deleting them, removes archived rows from BM25/vector retrieval, and supports unarchive. The save-time reconsolidation path also archives older near-duplicates rather than physically deleting them.
    51	- **Why it matters**: For long-lived systems, “forgetting” should usually mean reversible removal from active retrieval, not destruction. That preserves lineage, auditability, and recovery when the system over-prunes.
    52	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
    53	- **Impact**: high
    54	
    55	### Finding 4: Hard expiry should stay opt-in and explicitly ephemeral
    56	- **Source**: [save/types.ts:139](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/types.ts#L139), [scope-governance.ts:207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L207), [scope-governance.ts:255](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L255), [scope-governance.ts:260](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L260), [README.md:710](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L710), [memory-governance.vitest.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts#L160); evidence type: both
    57	- **What it does**: Public supports explicit `retentionPolicy` and `deleteAfter`, and governed ingest rejects `ephemeral` saves without a future expiry. I found test/audit evidence for a `retention_sweep` action, but I did not locate a concrete runtime sweep implementation in this checkout.
    58	- **Why it matters**: This is the correct shape for hard expiry: explicit, narrow, and policy-driven. The gap is operational visibility, not the policy model itself.
    59	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
    60	- **Impact**: medium
    61

    72	
    73	RESEARCH BRIEF:
    74	# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 004-opencode-mnemosyne-main
    75	
    76	## 2. Role
    77	
    78	You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
    79	
    80	## 3. Task
    81	
    82	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
    83	
    84	## 4. Context
    85	
    86	### 4.1 System Description
    87	
    88	OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
    89	
    90	The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
    91	
    92	### 4.2 Cross-Phase Awareness Table
    93	
    94	| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
    95	|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
    96	| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
    97	| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 004 (memory) | Focus drift detection, scaffold, no-DB approach |
    98	| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 004 (BM25 search) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, query expansion |
    99	| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (BM25) | Focus vector search, plugin architecture, scoping |
   100	
   101	### 4.3 What This Repo Already Has
   102	
   103	`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
   104	
   105	What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
   106	
   107	## 5. Instructions
   108	
   109	1. Treat `/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main` as the pre-approved phase folder. Skip Gate 3, keep all writes inside this phase folder, and treat everything under `external/` as read-only.
   110	2. Start with the plugin entry point: read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts` from top to bottom before the README. Confirm how the OpenCode plugin is declared, how `ctx.directory` / `ctx.worktree` are resolved, how project names are normalized, and where collection initialization happens.
   111	3. Read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/package.json` immediately after `src/index.ts` to confirm packaging boundaries, plugin metadata, runtime assumptions, and whether the repo exposes any source beyond the wrapper.
   112	4. Trace the shared `mnemosyne(...args)` bridge in `src/index.ts`: `Bun.spawn`, `cwd` selection, stdout/stderr handling, exit-code failure behavior, missing-binary fallback, and the security implication of passing argv arrays instead of interpolated shell strings.
   113	5. Examine `memory_recall` and `memory_recall_global` in detail. Trace argument schemas, quoted search-string handling, project vs global flags, `--format plain`, and how the wrapper surfaces "No memories found" versus real backend output.
   114	6. Examine `memory_store` and `memory_store_global` in detail. Trace write semantics, lazy creation of the global collection, project collection auto-init, and what these choices imply for memory scoping, onboarding friction, and failure modes.
   115	7. Examine `memory_delete` in detail. Trace how document IDs are expected to flow from recall results into deletion, and what this says about memory lifecycle and hygiene expectations.
   116	8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.

Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.

   132	    ```
   133	
   134	## 6. Research Questions
   135	
   136	1. How is hybrid retrieval presented at the plugin boundary: what does the README claim about BM25, vector similarity, and Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and what parts of that pipeline are actually visible from the wrapper?
   137	2. Why does Mnemosyne use `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`, and what should `Code_Environment/Public` learn from that model choice even if Public already has other embedding-backed retrieval systems?
   138	3. How effective is Mnemosyne's project-vs-global scope split, and would a similar ergonomic separation improve Public's memory-save and recall flows without duplicating existing scoped retrieval features?
   139	4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
   140	5. What operational value comes from the plugin auto-initializing the project collection and lazily initializing the global collection, and what failure or ambiguity risks come with deriving the project key from the directory basename?
   141	6. How should the README's AGENTS.md recommendation be interpreted as workflow guidance: is it merely usage advice, or does it reveal a reusable memory-discipline pattern for Public sessions?
   142	7. Compared with CocoIndex's semantic code search, what is unique about Mnemosyne's hybrid memory retrieval story, and where should those systems stay separate rather than merged?
   143	8. Compared with Public's current `memory_search` pipeline, which Mnemosyne ideas are already covered, which are partial overlaps, and which are genuinely new?
   144	9. How does the OpenCode plugin lifecycle shape adoption compared with MCP-server-based memory systems: installation, runtime assumptions, binary dependency management, and tool exposure ergonomics?
   145	10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?
   146	
   147	## 7. Do's
   148	
   149	- Do trace the full wrapper lifecycle in `src/index.ts`, not just the README summary.
   150	- Do separate wrapper-confirmed behavior from backend claims documented only in `README.md`.
   151	- Do trace the hybrid-search narrative end to end as a contract: query entry, quoted search handoff, backend search call, and fused-ranking claims.
   152	- Do study project and global scoping as product decisions, not just CLI flags.
   153	- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   154	- Do compare against current Public files so the research reflects today's hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior.
   155	- Do note where the external repo has no extra architecture files or AGENTS file, and treat that absence as evidence about how thin the wrapper really is.
   156	
   157	## 8. Don'ts
   158	
   159	- Do not spend most of the analysis on Mnemosyne's unseen Go internals; they are a dependency, not the plugin repo under study.
   160	- Do not conflate the TypeScript wrapper with the backend search implementation. State clearly when a claim is README-level rather than source-proven.
   161	- Do not ignore the compaction hook; it is the most novel and transferable feature for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   162	- Do not claim Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval when `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` already proves otherwise.
   163	- Do not try to run the system unless the binary and model are already installed; static analysis is sufficient for this phase, and first-run model download is large.
   164	- Do not collapse this phase into generic FTS5 or BM25 discussion already owned by phases `001` and `003`.
   165	- Do not edit anything under `external/` or outside this phase folder.
   166	
   167	## 9. Examples
   168	
   169	### Example A: Hybrid-search contract finding
   170	
   171	```text
   172	**Finding: Hybrid retrieval is a documented backend contract, not a wrapper-owned algorithm**
   173	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md; external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts
   174	- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
   175	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
   176	- Recommendation: prototype later
   177	- Affected area: Spec Kit Memory retrieval positioning, future plugin or wrapper design
   178	- Risk/cost: Medium; backend behavior is not directly inspectable from this repo
   179	```
   180	
   181	### Example B: Compaction-resilience finding
   182	
   183	```text
   184	**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
   185	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts; .opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js
   186	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
   187	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
   188	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
   189	- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
   190	- Risk/cost: Low to medium; prompt noise and duplicate instruction handling must be designed carefully
   191	```
   192	
   193	## 10. Constraints
   194	
   195	### 10.1 Error handling
   196	
   197	- If a claim is only present in the README and not in `src/index.ts`, label it as documented behavior, not source-confirmed implementation.
   198	- If current `Code_Environment/Public` behavior contradicts the phase brief, correct the comparison to match the code.
   199	- If the external repo structure differs from the assumed paths, preserve the same analytical order and document the actual files used.
   200	- If a recommendation depends on backend internals that are not visible here, mark that gap explicitly instead of guessing.
   201	
   202	### 10.2 Scope
   203	
   204	**IN SCOPE**
   205	
   206	- OpenCode plugin entry point and lifecycle
   207	- CLI bridge design and error handling
   208	- five memory tools and their ergonomics
   209	- project/global scoping model
   210	- documented hybrid search contract
   211	- AGENTS.md integration guidance from the README
   212	- compaction-hook behavior and comparison with Public
   213	- offline-first operational tradeoffs
   214	
   215	**OUT OF SCOPE**
   216	
   217	- reverse-engineering the unseen Mnemosyne Go ranking internals
   218	- generic TypeScript style commentary
   219	- plugin publishing workflow details
   220	- broad FTS5 or BM25 primers already covered by other phases
   221	- speculative replacement of Public's existing retrieval stack without evidence
   222	
   223	### 10.3 Prioritization framework
   224	
   225	Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
   226	
   227	## 11. Deliverables
   228	
   229	- `phase-research-prompt.md` present and tailored specifically to `004-opencode-mnemosyne-main`
   230	- `research/research.md` as the canonical report with at least 5 evidence-backed findings
   231	- explicit comparison against current Public files for hybrid retrieval, session recovery, compaction injection, and CocoIndex positioning
   232	- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   233	- each finding states whether evidence is wrapper-confirmed, README-documented, or both
   234	- `implementation-summary.md` created at the end
   235	- memory saved from this phase folder using `generate-context.js`
   236	
   237	Minimum finding schema:
   238	
   239	- finding title
   240	- exact source evidence
   241	- evidence type: wrapper-confirmed / README-documented / both
   242	- what OpenCode Mnemosyne does
   243	- why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`
   244	- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   245	- affected subsystem
   246	- migration, ambiguity, or validation risk
   247	
   248	## 12. Evaluation Criteria
   249	
   250	- CLEAR target: `>= 40/50`
   251	- RICCE compliance is visible in the prompt:
   252	  - **Role** is specialized in hybrid search, plugin architecture, scoping, and compaction resilience
   253	  - **Instructions** are ordered, concrete, path-specific, and start with the plugin entry point
   254	  - **Context** is domain-specific, cross-phase aware, and corrected to match current Public capabilities
   255	  - **Constraints** clearly separate wrapper evidence from README-level claims
   256	  - **Examples** show strong Mnemosyne-derived findings about hybrid search contracts and compaction hooks
   257	- at least 5 findings are evidence-backed rather than generic
   258	- cross-phase overlap with `001` and `003` is resolved explicitly
   259	- findings compare Mnemosyne with current Public hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior rather than a stale baseline
   260	- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly

    15	- **Source**: [archival-manager.ts:358](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L358), [archival-manager.ts:388](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L388), [archival-manager.ts:403](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L403), [archival-manager.ts:558](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L558), [archival-manager.ts:638](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L638), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L396); evidence type: wrapper-confirmed
    16	- **What it does**: Public archives low-value memories instead of immediately deleting them, removes archived rows from BM25/vector retrieval, and supports unarchive. The save-time reconsolidation path also archives older near-duplicates rather than physically deleting them.
    17	- **Why it matters**: For long-lived systems, “forgetting” should usually mean reversible removal from active retrieval, not destruction. That preserves lineage, auditability, and recovery when the system over-prunes.
    18	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
    19	- **Impact**: high

    22	- **Source**: [save/types.ts:139](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/types.ts#L139), [scope-governance.ts:207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L207), [scope-governance.ts:255](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L255), [scope-governance.ts:260](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L260), [README.md:710](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L710), [memory-governance.vitest.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts#L160); evidence type: both
    23	- **What it does**: Public supports explicit `retentionPolicy` and `deleteAfter`, and governed ingest rejects `ephemeral` saves without a future expiry. I found test/audit evidence for a `retention_sweep` action, but I did not locate a concrete runtime sweep implementation in this checkout.

    70	RESEARCH BRIEF:
    71	# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 004-opencode-mnemosyne-main
    72	
    73	## 2. Role
    74	
    75	You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
    76	
    77	## 3. Task
    78	
    79	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
    80	
    81	## 4. Context
    82	
    83	### 4.1 System Description
    84	
    85	OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
    86	
    87	The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
    88	
    89	### 4.2 Cross-Phase Awareness Table
    90	
    91	| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
    92	|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
    93	| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
    94	| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 004 (memory) | Focus drift detection, scaffold, no-DB approach |
    95	| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 004 (BM25 search) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, query expansion |
    96	| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (BM25) | Focus vector search, plugin architecture, scoping |
    97	
    98	### 4.3 What This Repo Already Has
    99	
   100	`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
   101	
   102	What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
   103	
   104	## 5. Instructions
   105	
   106	1. Treat `/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main` as the pre-approved phase folder. Skip Gate 3, keep all writes inside this phase folder, and treat everything under `external/` as read-only.

Confirm how the OpenCode plugin is declared, how `ctx.directory` / `ctx.worktree` are resolved, how project names are normalized, and where collection initialization happens.
   108	3. Read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/package.json` immediately after `src/index.ts` to confirm packaging boundaries, plugin metadata, runtime assumptions, and whether the repo exposes any source beyond the wrapper.
   109	4. Trace the shared `mnemosyne(...args)` bridge in `src/index.ts`: `Bun.spawn`, `cwd` selection, stdout/stderr handling, exit-code failure behavior, missing-binary fallback, and the security implication of passing argv arrays instead of interpolated shell strings.
   110	5. Examine `memory_recall` and `memory_recall_global` in detail. Trace argument schemas, quoted search-string handling, project vs global flags, `--format plain`, and how the wrapper surfaces "No memories found" versus real backend output.

Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
   115	10. Treat the Mnemosyne backend as an interface contract, not an implementation you can prove from this repo.    130	
   131	## 6. Research Questions
   132	
   133	1. How is hybrid retrieval presented at the plugin boundary: what does the README claim about BM25, vector similarity, and Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and what parts of that pipeline are actually visible from the wrapper?
   134	2. Why does Mnemosyne use `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`, and what should `Code_Environment/Public` learn from that model choice even if Public already has other embedding-backed retrieval systems?
   135	3. How effective is Mnemosyne's project-vs-global scope split, and would a similar ergonomic separation improve Public's memory-save and recall flows without duplicating existing scoped retrieval features?
   136	4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
   137	5. What operational value comes from the plugin auto-initializing the project collection and lazily initializing the global collection, and what failure or ambiguity risks come with deriving the project key from the directory basename?
   138	6. How should the README's AGENTS.md recommendation be interpreted as workflow guidance: is it merely usage advice, or does it reveal a reusable memory-discipline pattern for Public sessions?
   139	7. Compared with CocoIndex's semantic code search, what is unique about Mnemosyne's hybrid memory retrieval story, and where should those systems stay separate rather than merged?
   140	8. Compared with Public's current `memory_search` pipeline, which Mnemosyne ideas are already covered, which are partial overlaps, and which are genuinely new?
   141	9. How does the OpenCode plugin lifecycle shape adoption compared with MCP-server-based memory systems: installation, runtime assumptions, binary dependency management, and tool exposure ergonomics?
   142	10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?
   143	
   144	## 7. Do's
   145	
   146	- Do trace the full wrapper lifecycle in `src/index.ts`, not just the README summary.
   147	- Do separate wrapper-confirmed behavior from backend claims documented only in `README.md`.
   148	- Do trace the hybrid-search narrative end to end as a contract: query entry, quoted search handoff, backend search call, and fused-ranking claims.
   149	- Do study project and global scoping as product decisions, not just CLI flags.
   150	- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   151	- Do compare against current Public files so the research reflects today's hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior.
   152	- Do note where the external repo has no extra architecture files or AGENTS file, and treat that absence as evidence about how thin the wrapper really is.
   153	
   154	## 8. Don'ts
   155	
   156	- Do not spend most of the analysis on Mnemosyne's unseen Go internals; they are a dependency, not the plugin repo under study.
   157	- Do not conflate the TypeScript wrapper with the backend search implementation. State clearly when a claim is README-level rather than source-proven.
   158	- Do not ignore the compaction hook; it is the most novel and transferable feature for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   159	- Do not claim Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval when `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` already proves otherwise.
   160	- Do not try to run the system unless the binary and model are already installed; static analysis is sufficient for this phase, and first-run model download is large.
   161	- Do not collapse this phase into generic FTS5 or BM25 discussion already owned by phases `001` and `003`.
   162	- Do not edit anything under `external/` or outside this phase folder.
   163	
   164	## 9. Examples
   165	
   166	### Example A: Hybrid-search contract finding
   167	
   168	```text
   169	**Finding: Hybrid retrieval is a documented backend contract, not a wrapper-owned algorithm**
   170	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md; external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts
   171	- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
   172	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
   173	- Recommendation: prototype later
   174	- Affected area: Spec Kit Memory retrieval positioning, future plugin or wrapper design
   175	- Risk/cost: Medium; backend behavior is not directly inspectable from this repo
   176	```
   177	
   178	### Example B: Compaction-resilience finding
   179	
   180	```text
   181	**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
   182	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts; .opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js
   183	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
   184	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
   185	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
   186	- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
   187	- Risk/cost: Low to medium; prompt noise and duplicate instruction handling must be designed carefully
   188	```
   189	
   190	## 10. Constraints
   191	
   192	### 10.1 Error handling
   193	
   194	- If a claim is only present in the README and not in `src/index.ts`, label it as documented behavior, not source-confirmed implementation.
   195	- If current `Code_Environment/Public` behavior contradicts the phase brief, correct the comparison to match the code.
   196	- If the external repo structure differs from the assumed paths, preserve the same analytical order and document the actual files used.
   197	- If a recommendation depends on backend internals that are not visible here, mark that gap explicitly instead of guessing.
   198	
   199	### 10.2 Scope
   200	
   201	**IN SCOPE**
   202	
   203	- OpenCode plugin entry point and lifecycle
   204	- CLI bridge design and error handling
   205	- five memory tools and their ergonomics
   206	- project/global scoping model
   207	- documented hybrid search contract
   208	- AGENTS.md integration guidance from the README
   209	- compaction-hook behavior and comparison with Public
   210	- offline-first operational tradeoffs
   211	
   212	**OUT OF SCOPE**
   213	
   214	- reverse-engineering the unseen Mnemosyne Go ranking internals
   215	- generic TypeScript style commentary
   216	- plugin publishing workflow details
   217	- broad FTS5 or BM25 primers already covered by other phases
   218	- speculative replacement of Public's existing retrieval stack without evidence
   219	
   220	### 10.3 Prioritization framework
   221	
   222	Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
   223	
   224	## 11. Deliverables
   225	
   226	- `phase-research-prompt.md` present and tailored specifically to `004-opencode-mnemosyne-main`
   227	- `research/research.md` as the canonical report with at least 5 evidence-backed findings
   228	- explicit comparison against current Public files for hybrid retrieval, session recovery, compaction injection, and CocoIndex positioning
   229	- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   230	- each finding states whether evidence is wrapper-confirmed, README-documented, or both
   231	- `implementation-summary.md` created at the end
   232	- memory saved from this phase folder using `generate-context.js`
   233	
   234	Minimum finding schema:
   235

   237	- exact source evidence
   238	- evidence type: wrapper-confirmed / README-documented / both
   239	- what OpenCode Mnemosyne does
   240	- why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`
   241	- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   242	- affected subsystem
   243	- migration, ambiguity, or validation risk
   244	
   245	## 12. Evaluation Criteria
   246	
   247	- CLEAR target: `>= 40/50`
   248	- RICCE compliance is visible in the prompt:
   249	  - **Role** is specialized in hybrid search, plugin architecture, scoping, and compaction resilience
   250	  - **Instructions** are ordered, concrete, path-specific, and start with the plugin entry point
   251	  - **Context** is domain-specific, cross-phase aware, and corrected to match current Public capabilities

   257	- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly

    11	- **Why it matters**: This is the right architecture for long-lived memory. Permanence, recall probability, and current-session salience should stay independent so we do not accidentally make durable rules feel “old” or recent noise feel “important.”
    12	- **Recommendation**: adopt now

    15	### Finding 3: Reversible forgetting is stronger than hard deletion for durable memory
    16	- **Source**: [archival-manager.ts:358](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L358), [archival-manager.ts:388](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L388), [archival-manager.ts:403](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L403), [archival-manager.ts:558](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L558), [archival-manager.ts:638](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L638), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L396); evidence type: wrapper-confirmed
    17	- **What it does**: Public archives low-value memories instead of immediately deleting them, removes archived rows from BM25/vector retrieval, and supports unarchive. The save-time reconsolidation path also archives older near-duplicates rather than physically deleting them.
    18	- **Why it matters**: For long-lived systems, “forgetting” should usually mean reversible removal from active retrieval, not destruction. That preserves lineage, auditability, and recovery when the system over-prunes.
    19	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
    20	- **Impact**: high
    21	
    22	### Finding 4: Hard expiry should stay opt-in and explicitly ephemeral
    23	- **Source**: [save/types.ts:139](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/types.ts#L139), [scope-governance.ts:207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L207), [scope-governance.ts:255](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L255), [scope-governance.ts:260](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L260), [README.md:710](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md#L710), [memory-governance.vitest.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts#L160); evidence type: both
    24	- **What it does**: Public supports explicit `retentionPolicy` and `deleteAfter`, and governed ingest rejects `ephemeral` saves without a future expiry. I found test/audit evidence for a `retention_sweep` action, but I did not locate a concrete runtime sweep implementation in this checkout.
    25	- **Why it matters**: This is the correct shape for hard expiry: explicit, narrow, and policy-driven. The gap is operational visibility, not the policy model itself.
    26	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
    27	- **Impact**: medium
    28

    52	### Finding 6: The real missing benchmark is compaction continuity
    53	- **Source**: [index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
    54	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects memory-tool guidance during `experimental.session.compacting`. Public injects structural transport blocks and records bootstrap duration/completeness, but neither system turns post-compaction recovery into an explicit benchmark.
    55	- **Why it matters**: For long-running agent memory systems, the critical failure mode is often “after compaction, can the agent recover fast and correctly?”

    59	## Assessment
    60	- New information ratio: 0.68
    61	- The external system mostly measures execution success, not memory quality or retrieval accuracy. Public already has the stronger measurement stack.
    62	- The metrics we should track are:
    63	- Memory quality: `confidence`, `validationCount`, `positiveValidationCount`, negative-feedback rate, `access_count`, `last_accessed`, cleanup-candidate rate, archive/unarchive rate.
    64	- Retrieval accuracy: `Recall@K`, channel ablation delta, K-sensitivity, average result confidence, evidence-gap rate, `no_results` / `low_confidence` / `partial` distribution, stage latency breakdown.
    65	- System health: bootstrap duration/completeness, session quality score, graph freshness, embedding status counts, FTS parity drift, alias conflicts, retry stats, database size, startup projection consistency.
    66	- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `RESULT: PASSED` with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, plus the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
    67	
    68	## Recommended Next Focus
    69	Define a compaction-continuity benchmark for Public: measure recovery success after `experimental.session.compacting`, time to first successful `memory_context` / `memory_search`, and whether scoped/private/shared retrieval remains correct after bootstrap and compaction.

    71	RESEARCH BRIEF:
    72	# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 004-opencode-mnemosyne-main
    73	
    74	## 2. Role
    75	
    76	You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
    77	
    78	## 3. Task
    79	
    80	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
    81	
    82	## 4. Context
    83	
    84	### 4.1 System Description
    85	
    86	OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
    87	
    88	The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
    89	
    90	### 4.2 Cross-Phase Awareness Table
    91	
    92	| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
    93	|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
    94	| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
    95	| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 004 (memory) | Focus drift detection, scaffold, no-DB approach |
    96	| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 004 (BM25 search) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, query expansion |
    97	| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (BM25) | Focus vector search, plugin architecture, scoping |
    98	
    99	### 4.3 What This Repo Already Has
   100	
   101	`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
   102	
   103	What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
   104

Skip Gate 3, keep all writes inside this phase folder, and treat everything under `external/` as read-only.

   131	
   132	## 6. Research Questions
   133	
   134	1. How is hybrid retrieval presented at the plugin boundary: what does the README claim about BM25, vector similarity, and Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and what parts of that pipeline are actually visible from the wrapper?
   135	2. Why does Mnemosyne use `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`, and what should `Code_Environment/Public` learn from that model choice even if Public already has other embedding-backed retrieval systems?
   136	3. How effective is Mnemosyne's project-vs-global scope split, and would a similar ergonomic separation improve Public's memory-save and recall flows without duplicating existing scoped retrieval features?
   137	4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
   138	5. What operational value comes from the plugin auto-initializing the project collection and lazily initializing the global collection, and what failure or ambiguity risks come with deriving the project key from the directory basename?
   139	6. How should the README's AGENTS.md recommendation be interpreted as workflow guidance: is it merely usage advice, or does it reveal a reusable memory-discipline pattern for Public sessions?
   140	7. Compared with CocoIndex's semantic code search, what is unique about Mnemosyne's hybrid memory retrieval story, and where should those systems stay separate rather than merged?
   141	8. Compared with Public's current `memory_search` pipeline, which Mnemosyne ideas are already covered, which are partial overlaps, and which are genuinely new?
   142	9. How does the OpenCode plugin lifecycle shape adoption compared with MCP-server-based memory systems: installation, runtime assumptions, binary dependency management, and tool exposure ergonomics?
   143	10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?
   144	
   145	## 7. Do's
   146	
   147	- Do trace the full wrapper lifecycle in `src/index.ts`, not just the README summary.
   148	- Do separate wrapper-confirmed behavior from backend claims documented only in `README.md`.
   149	- Do trace the hybrid-search narrative end to end as a contract: query entry, quoted search handoff, backend search call, and fused-ranking claims.
   150	- Do study project and global scoping as product decisions, not just CLI flags.
   151	- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   152	- Do compare against current Public files so the research reflects today's hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior.
   153	- Do note where the external repo has no extra architecture files or AGENTS file, and treat that absence as evidence about how thin the wrapper really is.
   154	
   155	## 8. Don'ts
   156	
   157	- Do not spend most of the analysis on Mnemosyne's unseen Go internals; they are a dependency, not the plugin repo under study.
   158	- Do not conflate the TypeScript wrapper with the backend search implementation. State clearly when a claim is README-level rather than source-proven.
   159	- Do not ignore the compaction hook; it is the most novel and transferable feature for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   160	- Do not claim Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval when `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` already proves otherwise.
   161	- Do not try to run the system unless the binary and model are already installed; static analysis is sufficient for this phase, and first-run model download is large.
   162	- Do not collapse this phase into generic FTS5 or BM25 discussion already owned by phases `001` and `003`.
   163	- Do not edit anything under `external/` or outside this phase folder.
   164	
   165	## 9. Examples
   166	
   167	### Example A: Hybrid-search contract finding
   168	
   169	```text
   170	**Finding: Hybrid retrieval is a documented backend contract, not a wrapper-owned algorithm**
   171	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md; external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts
   172	- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
   173	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
   174	- Recommendation: prototype later
   175	- Affected area: Spec Kit Memory retrieval positioning, future plugin or wrapper design
   176	- Risk/cost: Medium; backend behavior is not directly inspectable from this repo
   177	```
   178	
   179	### Example B: Compaction-resilience finding
   180	
   181	```text
   182	**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
   183	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts; .opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js
   184	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
   185	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
   186	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
   187	- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
   188	- Risk/cost: Low to medium; prompt noise and duplicate instruction handling must be designed carefully
   189	```
   190	
   191	## 10. Constraints
   192	
   193	### 10.1 Error handling
   194	
   195	- If a claim is only present in the README and not in `src/index.ts`, label it as documented behavior, not source-confirmed implementation.
   196	- If current `Code_Environment/Public` behavior contradicts the phase brief, correct the comparison to match the code.
   197	- If the external repo structure differs from the assumed paths, preserve the same analytical order and document the actual files used.
   198	- If a recommendation depends on backend internals that are not visible here, mark that gap explicitly instead of guessing.
   199	
   200	### 10.2 Scope
   201	
   202	**IN SCOPE**
   203	
   204	- OpenCode plugin entry point and lifecycle
   205	- CLI bridge design and error handling
   206	- five memory tools and their ergonomics
   207	- project/global scoping model
   208	- documented hybrid search contract
   209	- AGENTS.md integration guidance from the README
   210	- compaction-hook behavior and comparison with Public
   211	- offline-first operational tradeoffs
   212	
   213	**OUT OF SCOPE**
   214	
   215	- reverse-engineering the unseen Mnemosyne Go ranking internals
   216	- generic TypeScript style commentary
   217	- plugin publishing workflow details
   218	- broad FTS5 or BM25 primers already covered by other phases
   219	- speculative replacement of Public's existing retrieval stack without evidence
   220	
   221	### 10.3 Prioritization framework
   222	
   223	Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
   224	
   225	## 11. Deliverables
   226	
   227	- `phase-research-prompt.md` present and tailored specifically to `004-opencode-mnemosyne-main`
   228	- `research/research.md` as the canonical report with at least 5 evidence-backed findings
   229	- explicit comparison against current Public files for hybrid retrieval, session recovery, compaction injection, and CocoIndex positioning
   230	- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   231	- each finding states whether evidence is wrapper-confirmed, README-documented, or both
   232	- `implementation-summary.md` created at the end
   233	- memory saved from this phase folder using `generate-context.js`
   234	
   235	Minimum finding schema:
   236	
   237	- finding title
   238	- exact source evidence
   239	- evidence type: wrapper-confirmed / README-documented / both
   240	- what OpenCode Mnemosyne does
   241	- why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`
   242	- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   243	- affected subsystem
   244	- migration, ambiguity, or validation risk
   245	
   246	## 12. Evaluation Criteria
   247	
   248	- CLEAR target: `>= 40/50`
   249	- RICCE compliance is visible in the prompt:
   250	  - **Role** is specialized in hybrid search, plugin architecture, scoping, and compaction resilience
   251	  - **Instructions** are ordered, concrete, path-specific, and start with the plugin entry point
   252	  - **Context** is domain-specific, cross-phase aware, and corrected to match current Public capabilities

   258	- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly
   259
